rationale: The citing paper uses Mitra and Golder (2002) in its theory section to support the claim that economically similar countries communicate and learn more from each other, which underpins expected spillover strength. It also uses the cited paper in the measurement setup, operationalizing economic distance as the absolute difference in log GDP per capita with an explicit reference to Mitra and Golder. This is more than a passing literature-list mention because the citation informs both conceptual justification and a concrete model variable.
relation_description: The citing study applies an idea from Mitra and Golder (2002) about near-market economic similarity to its own cross-country takeoff framework. Specifically, it transfers that logic to justify and construct the economic distance moderator in the spillover hazard model.
